response:
Sec. 1  
People who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their allergic reaction is triggered by the ingestion or contact with peanut proteins not by their environment  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to peanut proteins if they are present in the water or if the individual consumes food that contains peanuts  
Allergic reactions are immune system responses that occur regardless of the location in which the allergen is encountered  

Sec. 2  
The inability to live underwater is not related to the nature of the allergy itself but rather to the practical challenges of living in an aquatic environment  
Peanut allergies do not affect the ability to survive in water but they do pose a risk if the individual is exposed to peanuts while underwater  
Therefore the question is based on a misunderstanding of how allergies function and the conditions under which they are triggered
